title: galium austriacum subsp. balatonense
intro: Galium austriacum subsp. balatonense, also known as Hungarian bedstraw, is a subspecies of perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and improving digestion, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. Galium austriacum subsp. balatonense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has also been used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
